perturbation.original_text: 1.9 "ELEPHANT TALK Customer" shall mean any company or legal entity partnering with ELEPHANT TALK, which wishes to offer mobile services in a MVNO/ESP/SP model using the T-Mobile Network and the by T-Mobile provided Hosting Services under the terms and conditions as mentioned in this Agreement. ELEPHANT TALK Customers might include the mobile end-users of ELEPHANT TALK Customers, subject to the interpretation of the specific provision.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.9 "ELEPHANT TALK Customer" shall mean any company or legal entity partnering with ELEPHANT TALK, which wishes to offer mobile services in a MVNO/ESP/SP model using the T-Mobile Network and the by T-Mobile provided Hosting Services under the terms and conditions as mentioned in this Agreement. ELEPHANT TALK Clients include the mobile end-users of ELEPHANT TALK Customers, subject to the interpretation of the specific provision.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'ELEPHANT TALK Customers' to 'ELEPHANT TALK Clients' within the definition introduces ambiguity and a possible in-text contradiction. While initially defining 'ELEPHANT TALK Customer', the modified text uses 'ELEPHANT TALK Clients' to refer to the mobile end-users, implying that 'Customer' and 'Client' are interchangeable. However, this is not explicitly defined, creating uncertainty whether the terms 'Client' and 'Customer' refer to the same party or distinct entities. This could create confusion regarding the parties the 'ELEPHANT TALK Customer' interacts with.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.7 ELEPHANT TALK shall purchase the Hosting Services from T-Mobile as an independent contractor. ELEPHANT TALK shall sell and market the ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Services to ELEPHANT TALK Customers in its own name, for its own account and on its own risk (subject to the terms of this Agreement). ELEPHANT TALK warrants T-Mobile that ELEPHANT TALK Customers shall sell and market their mobile telecommunication services based on the ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Services in its own name, for its own account and on its own risk. ELEPHANT TALK and ELEPHANT TALK Customers shall not in any respect represent T-Mobile or enter into any agreement or other commitment on T-Mobile's behalf.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces the term 'ELEPHANT TALK Clients' in the definition of 'ELEPHANT TALK Customer'. The contradicted text refers exclusively to 'ELEPHANT TALK Customers' in the context of selling and marketing services and warrants T-Mobile that 'ELEPHANT TALK Customers' will market services. By using 'ELEPHANT TALK Clients' within the definition, the modified text creates ambiguity. It is unclear whether the obligations related to 'ELEPHANT TALK Customers' in section 2.7 also apply to 'ELEPHANT TALK Clients', potentially undermining the commitments made by ELEPHANT TALK on behalf of its customers. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses the terms 'Customers' and 'Clients' interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations regarding who is responsible for selling and marketing services.

perturbation.original_text: 1.15 "Hosting Service(s)" shall mean the network hosting (and related) services - as listed in Appendix 1 - provided by T-Mobile to ELEPHANT TALK under the Agreement, enabling ELEPHANT TALK to provide ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Services to ELEPHANT TALK Customers in accordance with its terms and conditions of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.15 "Hosting Service(s)" shall mean the network hosting (and related) services - as listed in Appendix 1 - provided by T-Mobile to ELEPHANT TALK under the Agreement, enabling ELEPHANT TALK to provide ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Solutions to ELEPHANT TALK Customers in accordance with its terms and conditions of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Services' to 'ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Solutions' creates a contradiction because the original definition uses 'Services', and subsequently the definition is changed to use 'Solutions'. This introduces uncertainty as to whether 'Services' and 'Solutions' are considered the same thing, because this term is never formally defined as 'Solutions' in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.7 ELEPHANT TALK shall purchase the Hosting Services from T-Mobile as an independent contractor. ELEPHANT TALK shall sell and market the ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Services to ELEPHANT TALK Customers in its own name, for its own account and on its own risk (subject to the terms of this Agreement). ELEPHANT TALK warrants T-Mobile that ELEPHANT TALK Customers shall sell and market their mobile telecommunication services based on the ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Services in its own name, for its own account and on its own risk. ELEPHANT TALK and ELEPHANT TALK Customers shall not in any respect represent T-Mobile or enter into any agreement or other commitment on T-Mobile's behalf.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Services' to 'ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Solutions' creates a contradiction. Section 2.7 explicitly refers to 'ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Services'. By redefining the term in 1.15 to use 'Solutions' instead of 'Services', the contract introduces ambiguity and a potential contradiction because it's unclear whether the obligations and rights described in section 2.7 now apply to 'Solutions' as well. This ambiguity would impact the enforceability and interpretation of the contract, specifically regarding Elephant Talk's obligations to market and sell to ELEPHANT TALK Customers.

perturbation.original_text: 4.4 ***. T-Mobile shall resolve every critical part of the Hosting Service as soon as reasonable possible and in any event before the date which is *** after the agreed ***. Once the Hosting Service and every critical part, as *** the PoD, thereof has successfully passed the *** pursuant to the procedures described in the PoD and Appendix 3 the Service shall be accepted by ELEPHANT TALK and ELEPHANT TALK shall sign the Protocol of Delivery acknowledging such acceptance.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.4 ***. T-Mobile shall resolve every critical part of the Hosting Solution as soon as reasonable possible and in any event before the date which is *** after the agreed ***. Once the Hosting Service and every critical part, as *** the PoD, thereof has successfully passed the *** pursuant to the procedures described in the PoD and Appendix 3 the Service shall be accepted by ELEPHANT TALK and ELEPHANT TALK shall sign the Protocol of Delivery acknowledging such acceptance.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Hosting Service' to 'Hosting Solution' in the first sentence of this section creates a contradiction. Previously, 'Hosting Service(s)' was formally defined, where as 'Hosting Solution' has not been. This creates uncertainty as to whether 'Service' and 'Solution' are interchangeable. Because the original text does not define the two as related, it could cause parties to dispute the meaning of the two in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 ***. T-Mobile shall resolve every critical part of the Hosting Solution as soon as reasonable possible and in any event before the date which is *** after the agreed ***. Once the Hosting Service and every critical part, as *** the PoD, thereof has successfully passed the *** pursuant to the procedures described in the PoD and Appendix 3 the Service shall be accepted by ELEPHANT TALK and ELEPHANT TALK shall sign the Protocol of Delivery acknowledging such acceptance.
perturbation.justification: The modification replaces 'Hosting Service' with 'Hosting Solution' in the Changed Text, while the Contradicted Text uses both terms. The Perturbation Explanation states that 'Hosting Service' is a defined term, whereas 'Hosting Solution' is not, thus creating ambiguity and potentially conflicting interpretations. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, indicating a strong in-text contradiction due to the use of undefined terminology in a way that impacts the defined 'Hosting Service'.
